stories.headline: Rashee Rice walks free, dodges the noose
stories.read: Well pardner, the Chiefs' Rashee Rice stepped out of that Dallas jail after his 30-day stint, and the league's shown no appetite to hang another suspension on him. That means a full slate of games with Mahomes slingin' it his way. His knee's mendin' and Andy Reid says he reports to camp on time, so the dry well everybody feared just turned back into a gushin' vein. If your saloon's still pricin' him like damaged goods, go stake that claim before the herd wises up.

stories.headline: A.J. Brown rides east, leaves a creek wide open in Philly
stories.read: The Eagles shipped A.J. Brown out and now there's a whole pile of throws left layin' in the dirt — somethin' like 131 vacated targets after Marquise Brown lit out too. Brown's a No. 1 again up in New England, but on a greener offense, so temper that ceiling, pardner. The real strike is every remaining Philly pass-catcher just got a fatter cut of the throws. Go pan that creek before your leaguemates even notice the gold.

stories.headline: Pickens saddles up in Dallas, no holdout in sight
stories.read: George Pickens reported to Cowboys minicamp on his $27.3 million tag and told the wire he ain't holdin' out — football first, contract later. That's a clean green light: a downfield burner gettin' a full run of work with Dak handin' him the reins. No camp drama means no fog over his value come the great land grab. Lock in your read now while folks are still spooked by the tag talk.
